mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.2% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.2%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and falling capacity utilization, leading to negative earnings revisions and lower S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in >58% of cases, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent predictive power in validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll growth directly increases aggregate income and corporate profit margins, generating positive forward equity returns through higher expected cash flows.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or Rank IC remains negative over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5% signals economic contraction, reducing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.5% is followed by positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates robust economic momentum without labor market overheating, supporting positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in >65% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation above 3.0% during a post-shock recovery phase forces markets to price in delayed rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3.0% during recovery phases yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or regime-specific predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample tests.
